trade.summary: The Australia-UK Free Trade Agreement (AUKFTA), signed in 2021 and entering into force in 2023, liberalised trade substantially. Australia exports beef, wine, services, and resources; the UK exports vehicles, pharmaceuticals, financial services, and machinery. The FTA eliminates tariffs on virtually all goods and expands services market access.

diplomatic.summary: Australia and the UK share the deepest possible relationship: a common sovereign (His Majesty the King is head of state of both countries), Five Eyes intelligence partnership, AUKUS trilateral security pact, Commonwealth membership, and deeply aligned values and institutions. The relationship is described as a 'living alliance' with continuous high-level engagement at all government levels.

military.summary: The pinnacle of the bilateral relationship. The UK is a founding AUKUS partner alongside Australia and the US, committing to deliver nuclear-powered submarines to Australia. Five Eyes intelligence sharing operates seamlessly. Joint military exercises are frequent and comprehensive. Both nations have fought together in every major conflict of the 20th and 21st centuries. The UK's Carrier Strike Group visited Australian ports in 2021.

financial.summary: The UK is one of the largest sources of FDI into Australia and vice versa. Deep integration of financial markets, with ASX and LSE-listed companies overlapping significantly. Australian superannuation funds are major investors in UK infrastructure. The City of London is a primary destination for Australian financial services.

energy.summary: The UK is interested in Australian LNG and green hydrogen as part of energy security diversification. Growing cooperation on offshore wind and clean energy technology. Australia supplies uranium to UK civil nuclear facilities.

scientific.summary: Extraordinary depth of scientific collaboration. Australian and British universities have extensive joint programs. CSIRO and UK Research and Innovation (UKRI) maintain formal partnership agreements. Joint research spans health, climate, AI, space, and agriculture. Mutual recognition of professional qualifications facilitates researcher mobility.
